home.headline: Preset Forge
home.subtitle: Edit, share, and sync Valeton GP-200 presets live via USB MIDI. Import Line6 HX Stomp .hlx presets.

footer.disclaimer: Preset Forge — Unofficial tool. Not affiliated with Valeton.

device.connectHint: Connect via USB MIDI to your Valeton GP-200. Load, edit, and send presets live to the device.
device.versionWarning: Only tested with FW 1.2 — other versions at your own risk
device.firmwareBannerText: This app was only tested with firmware 1.2. Your version ({version}) may cause issues.
device.firmwareCompatInfo: This app was only tested with firmware {versions}. Your device has firmware {detected}.
device.firmwareCompatRisk: With untested firmware, presets may get corrupted or the device may behave unexpectedly.

help.title: Help
help.gettingStartedLoad: Load a .prst file by dragging it into the editor or clicking "Load preset from disk".
help.gettingStartedEdit: Click on an effect slot to adjust parameters. Toggle effects on or off with the LED button. Switch between pedal view and list view.
help.gettingStartedSave: Click "Save preset as file" to download your edited preset as a .prst file.
help.gettingStartedReorder: Drag and drop effect slots to reorder the signal chain.
help.gettingStartedAuthor: Each .prst file contains an author name (max 16 characters). You can view and edit it in the editor.
help.hxStompLoad: Load a .hlx file from a Line6 HX Stomp. The app automatically converts it to GP-200 format.
help.hxStompExperimental: This feature is experimental. Not all HX Stomp effects have a GP-200 equivalent — unmapped effects are skipped or approximated.
help.hxStompWhat: Converted presets include effect types, parameters, and signal chain order. Review the result in the editor before saving.
help.usbMidiBrowser: Web MIDI is only supported in Chrome and Edge. Firefox and Safari are not compatible.
help.usbMidiConnect: Connect your GP-200 via USB and click "Connect GP-200". The browser will ask for permission to access the MIDI device.
help.usbMidiLive: Changes in the editor are sent to the device instantly: toggling effects, changing parameters, and reordering the signal chain.
help.usbMidiPullPush: Use "Load" to pull a preset from the device and "Save" to push it back.
help.galleryUpload: Sign in and upload presets to your profile. Publish them to the gallery so others can discover them.
help.galleryShare: Each preset gets a unique share link. You can reset the link at any time to revoke access.
help.galleryDownload: Browse the gallery for presets from other users and download them directly. Filter by style or module type.
help.bankNavTabs: When you load a preset via USB, the entire bank (A/B/C/D) is loaded. Switch between the four slots using the tabs.
help.bankNavPrevNext: Use the arrow buttons on the left and right of the tabs to navigate to the previous or next bank.
help.playlistsCreate: Create playlists with songs for your practice sessions or live performances. Each song can have multiple presets assigned.
help.playlistsSlots: Select device slots from your GP-200 to associate with each preset in a song. Presets are pushed to the device when activated.
help.playlistsCuePoints: Add timed cue points to songs: at a specific timestamp, the app can automatically switch presets or toggle effects on the device.
help.playlistsCountIn: A 3-2-1 count-in is shown before each cue point triggers, so you can prepare for the preset change.
help.playlistsReconnect: If the USB connection drops during a performance, the app will attempt to auto-reconnect without losing your playlist position.
help.shortcutsPlaylistUpDown: Arrow Up / Arrow Down — previous / next song in the playlist player.
help.shortcutsPlaylistLeftRight: Arrow Left / Arrow Right — previous / next preset within the current song.
help.faqBrowserA: The editor works in all modern browsers. For USB MIDI connections, Chrome or Edge is required.
help.faqFirmwareA: The app was tested with firmware 1.8.0. Other versions may work but are not officially supported.
help.faqFormatA: The .prst format is the proprietary preset file format of the Valeton GP-200. Each file is exactly 1224 bytes and contains 11 effect slots with up to 15 parameters each.
help.faqOfflineA: Yes — Preset Forge is a PWA and works offline for editing presets. Gallery, profile, and playlist features require an internet connection.
help.faqHxStompQ: Can I import Line6 HX Stomp presets?
help.faqHxStompA: Yes, experimentally. Load a .hlx file in the editor and the app will convert it to GP-200 format. Not all effects have a direct equivalent, so check the result.

legal.responsible: Information pursuant to § 5 TMG (German Teleservices Act)
legal.contentResponsibleText: Manuel Rödig (address as above) — responsible pursuant to § 55 para. 2 RStV.
legal.liabilityContentText: The contents of this website have been created with the greatest care. However, we cannot guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the content. As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages under general law pursuant to § 7 para. 1 TMG. According to §§ 8–10 TMG, however, we are not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information.
legal.liabilityLinksText: Our website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. The respective provider is always responsible for the content of the linked pages. Permanent content monitoring of linked pages is unreasonable without concrete indications of a legal violation.
legal.copyrightText: The content and works on these pages created by the site operator are subject to German copyright law. The software is open source and available under the MIT license on GitHub. Preset files uploaded by users remain the property of the respective users.
legal.trademarkText: Valeton and GP-200 are registered trademarks of Valeton Technology Co., Ltd. Preset Forge is an independent community project and is not affiliated with Valeton. The use of trademark names serves solely to describe compatibility.
legal.privacyOverviewText: Personal data is collected on this website only to the extent technically necessary. Data processing is carried out on the basis of the GDPR (Art. 6 para. 1). We do not share personal data with third parties unless required by law.
legal.privacyRightsText: You have the right to free information about your stored data (Art. 15 GDPR), to rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction of processing (Art. 18), data portability (Art. 20), and the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77) at any time.
legal.privacyServerLogsText: The hosting provider automatically collects and stores information in server log files: browser type, operating system, referrer URL, hostname, time of request, and IP address. This data is collected on the basis of Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR and is not merged with other data sources.
legal.privacyCookiesText: This website uses a session cookie (auth_session) for authentication. This cookie is technically necessary and is deleted when you sign out. No tracking cookies or third-party cookies are used.
legal.privacyAccountText: When you register, your email address, username, and a hashed password (Argon2id) are stored. You may optionally add a profile picture, bio, and website URL. You can view and modify all your data in your profile at any time. To delete your account, please contact us by email.
legal.privacyStorageText: Uploaded preset files are stored encrypted on our own S3-compatible storage (Garage) in Germany. Preset metadata (name, description, tags) is stored in the database. You can download and delete your presets at any time.
legal.privacyAnalyticsText: This website uses Matomo, a privacy-friendly open-source web analytics tool, operated on our own servers. IP addresses are anonymized. No data is shared with third parties. You can opt out of the analysis at any time.
legal.privacyEmailText: We only send emails for account verification and password reset purposes. Your email address is not used for newsletters or advertising and is not shared with third parties.
legal.privacySslText: This site uses SSL/TLS encryption for security. You can recognize an encrypted connection by the "https://" prefix in your browser's address bar.
